channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Middle East conflict causes a fluoride shortage for US drinking water
item.description: Some U.S. water systems are cutting back on fluoride because of a key chemical is in short supply. Israel is one of its main producers.
item.content:encoded:
Some U.S. water systems are cutting back on fluoride because of a key chemical is in short supply. Israel is one of its main producers.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Popes have spoken out on politics before. But with Trump and Pope Leo it's different
item.description: President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.

(Image credit: Alberto Pizzoli)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Visionary NPR leader Kevin Klose, who led network to new heights, has died
item.description: Klose led NPR for a decade starting in 1998, a period of incredible growth for the public media network.
item.content:encoded:
Klose led NPR for a decade starting in 1998, a period of incredible growth for the public media network.

(Image credit: Jacques Coughlin/Jacques Coughlin)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Once again, Trump threatens to fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell
item.description: President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Greetings from Nairobi, where taking a matatu is no ordinary bus ride
item.description: The moment you board, the music grabs you. These privately owned, brightly painted minibuses are moving canvases, mobile sound systems — rolling declarations of what young Nairobi finds cool.
item.content:encoded: The moment you board, the music grabs you. These privately owned, brightly painted minibuses are moving canvases, mobile sound systems — rolling declarations of what young Nairobi finds cool.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Igunza

item.title: Trump administration moves to erase Jan. 6 riot convictions for seditious conspiracy
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to vacate the seditious conspiracy convictions of extremists involved in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack who earlier received commutations instead of full pardons.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is moving to vacate the seditious conspiracy convictions of extremists involved in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack who earlier received commutations instead of full pardons.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach

item.title: Trump says new talks with Iran will happen soon. And, Eric Swalwell faces new allegations
item.description: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump says Iran talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
item.description: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Fela Kuti is the first African artist to enter the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.
item.content:encoded:
In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.

(Image credit: Leni Sinclair/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ian Brennan

item.title: Tax season was supposed to bring big refunds. So far they're less than expected
item.description: The average refund so far is $350 more than last year at this time, despite projections that it would be closer to $1,000 due to Republican-led tax changes as part of the Big Beautiful Bill Act.
item.content:encoded:
The average refund so far is $350 more than last year at this time, despite projections that it would be closer to $1,000 due to Republican-led tax changes as part of the Big Beautiful Bill Act.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
